name: ds-checkbox
description: Binary toggle control. Reflects name/checked/disabled; reports state changes as ds-change.

events.name: ds-change
events.description: Fired when the checked state toggles, via pointer or keyboard (Space).

constraints.rationale: No primitive references, no raw values.

The checkmark's foreground/background pairing (action-bg/action-fg, already a declared ds.contrastPair) must clear AA in both contexts; a standalone instance must carry an accessible name; never a positive tabindex.
Adaptive class: API drift is structural drift.

provenance.author: ai
provenance.justification: Design decision: checked-state color reuses --sem-color-action-bg / --sem-color-action-fg (the same pair ds-button consumes) rather than introducing a dedicated checkbox accent token. Rejected alternative: a separate --sem-color-accent-* pair scoped to selection controls, which would read more intentionally in a mature system. Rejected for the POC because it is a tokens.json edit outside this AI-authored definition's authority, and because reusing the action pair keeps 'the thing that means affirmative/selected' visually consistent between a pressed button and a checked box without adding token surface or a new untested contrast pair. Keyboard activation (Space toggles, matching native <input type=checkbox> behavior) is wired the same way ds-button wires Enter/Space activation, rather than relying on this POC's headless environment to exercise native checkbox semantics, which happy-dom does not reliably simulate.
